title: Analysts Warn of 'April Oil Cliff' — Global Buffer Reserves Expiring by Mid-April
detail: Multiple major energy research firms issued warnings on March 30 that the buffer from strategic petroleum reserve releases, Russian oil, and Iranian sanctions-exempt flows will run out by mid-April — creating an 'April oil cliff.' BCA Research's Marko Papic warned the supply loss will 'double by mid-April, becoming the largest loss of crude supply' in modern history, with few government levers remaining. Rystad Energy stated the global system has shifted 'from buffered to fragile.' Goldman Sachs described the disruption as 'the largest shock in decades when measured as a share of global supply.' The IEA called it 'the biggest oil shock in history' — approximately 17.8–20 million barrels per day disrupted, with approximately 500 million barrels total lost since February 28. Brent crude hovered near $115/barrel as of March 30. April 6 remains the Trump hard deadline for full Hormuz reopening.

title: Iran's Hardline Lawmakers Push NPT Withdrawal Following Arak and Natanz Strikes
detail: Iranian hardline lawmakers escalated pressure on the government on March 30 to withdraw Iran from the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), following Israeli-US strikes on the Arak heavy water reactor, the Natanz enrichment facility, the Fordow deep facility, and the Yazd/Ardakan yellowcake plants. The NPT exit campaign is framed as both a deterrence signal — Iran would be free of IAEA inspections — and a retaliatory political gesture against the international community's perceived acquiescence in the dismantlement of Iran's nuclear program. The IAEA's Board of Governors convened an emergency session on March 30 to assess the status of Iran's nuclear facilities following four weeks of strikes. Iran's UN ambassador reiterated that the attacks constitute 'a war crime and a crime against humanity.'

title: US Airstrikes Kill PMF Fighters at Kirkuk Airport; 3 Killed in Mosul Rashidiya
detail: US airstrikes targeted Pro-Iranian Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) sites in Iraq on March 29-30, killing 2 police officers and PMF fighters at Kirkuk International Airport and 3 PMF fighters in Mosul's Rashidiya area, and additional fighters in Tuz Khurmatu (Salah ad-Din province). The strikes reflect ongoing US efforts to suppress Iranian proxy activity in Iraq that could threaten US bases and supply lines. The Iraqi government formally protested the strikes as a violation of Iraqi sovereignty. PMF groups have continued to provide Iran with a secondary front, absorbing US military attention and resources alongside the primary Iran theater.

title: UNIFIL Peacekeeper Killed in Southern Lebanon — UN Demands Protection
detail: A United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) peacekeeper was killed in an attack on a UN position in southern Lebanon on or around March 29-30. UN Secretary-General Guterres issued an urgent statement demanding all parties cease attacks on UNIFIL positions and personnel, calling the death 'an unacceptable escalation.' The killing marks one of the most direct impacts of the Lebanon front on international peacekeeping forces deployed since the 2006 war. The IDF maintained its declared 30km security zone to the Litani River and continued strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in the south despite the UNIFIL fatality.

title: Trump Economic Approval at 29% — Lowest Polling Amid War-Driven Inflation Pressure
detail: A Reuters/Ipsos poll released around March 30 showed Trump's economic approval rating at 29% — lower than Biden's record low — as the Iran war's economic impacts (gas prices up 69%, Brent crude at $115, LNG disruption, 36,000+ cancelled flights) continued to hit US consumers. The survey reflects growing domestic pressure on Trump to end the conflict ahead of November 2026 midterm elections. Analysts noted Trump is privately eager to claim a victory and exit the conflict but has been unable to secure Iran's formal surrender or full Hormuz reopening before the April 6 deadline. Several senior Republicans from oil-state constituencies issued public warnings about the economic consequences of extended conflict.

title: IDF Strikes Kill Six Iranian Diplomats at Residence in Lebanon
detail: Six Iranian diplomats were killed in an Israeli airstrike on their residence in Lebanon on approximately March 29-30. Iran's ambassador to Lebanon was subsequently declared persona non grata by Lebanese authorities. The killing of accredited diplomats represents a significant escalation in the targeting of Iranian state personnel and is a violation of the Vienna Convention protections for diplomatic staff. Iran's Foreign Ministry summoned all available ambassadors for an emergency consultation and called on the UN Security Council to convene urgently. Senior Hezbollah liaison Ayyoub Hussein Yaqoub and missile unit figure Yasser Mohammad Mubarak were also eliminated in related operations.
